title: Add support for --iidfile to bud and commit
body:
Add support for an --iidfile which specifies the file to write the
image id, when committing a new image.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: Not sure if specifying --idfile causes the ID To no longer be printed to screen.
My bad, should be '--iidfile' not '--idfile'.    image id file.

I will add tests, as soon as someone confirms if the ID Is still output to the terminal if you pass this command on docker build.  I don't have access to a machine running a new enough docker which support --iidfile.

Here is my execution result:
```
➜  testDockerfile sudo docker build --iidfile 123456 .
Sending build context to Docker daemon  2.048kB
Step 1/2 : FROM busybox
 ---> f6e427c148a7
Step 2/2 : MAINTAINER zhouhao "zhouhao@cn.fujitsu.com"
 ---> Running in 4fcf9623ec0b
Removing intermediate container 4fcf9623ec0b
 ---> 50e23ae53fbf
Successfully built 50e23ae53fbf
➜  testDockerfile ls   
123456  Dockerfile
➜  testDockerfile cat 123456 
sha256:50e23ae53fbf84e5b3273dc10c98e055d4b89c89cb6bc50df2c1a8d416b6b3aa%  

```

Here is my docker info:
```
➜  testDockerfile sudo docker version
Client:
 Version:	18.04.0-ce
 API version:	1.37
 Go version:	go1.10.1
 Git commit:	3d479c0af6
 Built:	Tue Apr 17 22:46:17 2018
 OS/Arch:	linux/amd64
 Experimental:	false
 Orchestrator:	swarm

Server:
 Engine:
  Version:	18.04.0-ce
  API version:	1.37 (minimum version 1.12)
  Go version:	go1.10.1
  Git commit:	3d479c0af6
  Built:	Tue Apr 17 22:46:48 2018
  OS/Arch:	linux/amd64
  Experimental:	false
```

I have a feeling that the CI failure isn't related to this change.
Test `commit-to-from-elsewhere` failed: 
```
error computing local image name for \"dir:/var/tmp/tmp.fjk_4.jp4oipp15./elsewhere-img\": error parsing computed local image name \"var/tmp/tmp.fjk_4.jp4oipp15./elsewhere-img\": error parsing named reference \"var/tmp/tmp.fjk_4.jp4oipp15./elsewhere-img\": invalid reference format
```
